nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

en.lifeSummary: Alice De Grey was born in 1380, in Wilton, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 daughter. She died in 1459, in Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice De Grey est née en 1380, à Wilton, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1459, à Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Alice De Grey wurde 1380 in Wilton, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1459 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Alice De Grey è nata il nel 1380 nel Wilton, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1459, nel Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice De Greyは1380年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィルトンで生まれた。彼女は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1459年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアカストレ-プウルバーバトチで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice De Grey(은)는 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 윌턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1459년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 카스틀레-풀버바트치에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.
